title: pulmonaria hirta subsp. apennina
intro: Pulmonaria hirta subsp. apennina, also known as Italian lungwort, is a subspecies of the lungwort plant, which is a type of flowering perennial native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. The plant is rich in glycosides and iridoids, which are believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pulmonaria hirta subsp. apennina include its potential to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Pulmonaria hirta subsp. apennina is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to limited research on its safety and efficacy.
